title: Songs Set To Music: 3. Set By Mr. De Fesch
content:
Strephonetta, why d'ye fly me,
With such rigour in your eyes:
Oh! 'tis cruel to deny me,
Since your charms I so much prize.

But I plainly see the reason
Why in vain I you pursued,
Her to gain 'twas out of season,
Who before the chaplain woo'd.
meaning:
"Songs Set To Music: 3. Set By Mr. De Fesch" is a quintessential example of Matthew Prior's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
